title: [lichen_l2.c:1806] rx_mutex held during potentially-blocking allocation
description:
What: lichen_l2_input() holds rx_mutex from line 1656 until line 1832, including during net_pkt_rx_alloc_with_buffer() which can block for CONFIG_LICHEN_L2_RX_ALLOC_TIMEOUT_MS (default 50ms).

Why it matters: If the system is under memory pressure:
1. RX callback enters, acquires rx_mutex
2. net_pkt allocation blocks for up to 50ms
3. Any other operation needing rx_mutex (peer_add, peer_remove, enable/disable) blocks

At LoRa data rates, this is unlikely to cause starvation, but the design holds the mutex longer than necessary.

Evidence: k_mutex_lock at 1656, net_pkt_rx_alloc at 1806-1808, k_mutex_unlock at 1832.
close_reason: Documented as known trade-off in lichen_l2.c. Comment explains: holding mutex during allocation prevents races, timeout bounds blocking to 50ms max, LoRa data rates make contention acceptable.
